info.title: Homebrew: openssl@1.1 not found error diagnosis

parts.text: I am seeing the following error when using zsh: "Error: No available formula with the name "openssl@1.1". Did you mean openssl@3.5, openssl@3.0 or openssl@3?". Can you help diagnose?

Yep — this usually means your shell/startup script (or a tool installer) is trying to use an old Homebrew package that no longer exists.

`openssl@1.1` was removed from Homebrew core after OpenSSL 1.1 reached end-of-life, so Brew can’t install it anymore.

Most likely causes:
- Your `~/.zshrc`, `~/.zprofile`, or `~/.profile` has lines referencing `openssl@1.1`
- A tool setup script (often `pyenv`, `rbenv`, old Node/Python/Ruby build instructions) runs `brew install openssl@1.1`

Quick diagnosis commands:
```bash
brew search openssl
grep -n "openssl@1.1\|openssl/1.1" ~/.zshrc ~/.zprofile ~/.profile ~/.zlogin 2>/dev/null
```

If found in shell config, replace with OpenSSL 3:
```bash
brew install openssl@3
```

And update env lines from something like:
```bash
export LDFLAGS="-L$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)/lib"
export CPPFLAGS="-I$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)/include"
```
to:
```bash
export LDFLAGS="-L$(brew --prefix openssl@3)/lib"
export CPPFLAGS="-I$(brew --prefix openssl@3)/include"
```

Then reload shell:
```bash
exec zsh
```

If you want, I can help you pinpoint exactly which tool in your setup still requires `openssl@1.1` and what modern replacement command to use.

state.title: Checks Homebrew prefix for openssl 1.1

parts.text:
Found it — your shell is executing an outdated OpenSSL reference in startup config.

- The exact source is `~/.zshenv:62`:
  - `export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)"`
- `openssl@1.1` is removed from Homebrew, so every new zsh session triggers that Brew error.
- I confirmed by running `brew --prefix openssl@1.1`, which returns the same error you pasted.

Fix:
1. Install current OpenSSL formula:
   - `brew install openssl@3`
2. Update `~/.zshenv:62` to:
   - `[[ "$unamestr" == 'Darwin' ]] && export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@3)"`
3. Reload shell:
   - `exec zsh`

If you want this to be more resilient, use this instead (falls back cleanly if formula names change):
```zsh
if [[ "$unamestr" == 'Darwin' ]]; then
  OPENSSL_PREFIX="$(brew --prefix openssl@3 2>/dev/null || brew --prefix openssl 2>/dev/null)"
  [[ -n "$OPENSSL_PREFIX" ]] && export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$OPENSSL_PREFIX"
fi
```

If you share your Ruby version (`ruby -v`), I can also tell you whether you should keep `RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS` at all or remove it.

Great data point — that Ruby version explains a lot.

- `ruby 2.6.10` is EOL, and OpenSSL 1.1 is also EOL.
- Your `RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS` line is only needed when **building Ruby from source** (e.g., `rbenv install`), not for normal shell use.
- So in your case, that line is safe to remove from global startup and is the direct cause of the zsh error.

Do this now:
```zsh
# edit ~/.zshenv and remove/comment this line:
# export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)"
exec zsh
```

Recommended path:
- Upgrade to a maintained Ruby (ideally `3.2` or `3.3`).
- If using rbenv, install with OpenSSL 3:
```zsh
brew install openssl@3 ruby-build rbenv
RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@3)" rbenv install 3.3.6
rbenv global 3.3.6
```

If you must keep 2.6 for legacy work, keep the OpenSSL setting scoped to that one install command (not in `~/.zshenv`) and expect compatibility friction on modern macOS.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/mark/.zshenv
@@
-# Use a version of OpenSSL that will be managed
-[[ "$unamestr" == 'Darwin' ]] && export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)"
-
 # rbenv shims
 [[ $(command -v rbenv) ]] && _extend_path "${HOME}/.rbenv/bin"
*** End Patch

input.command:
python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; p=Path('/Users/mark/.zshenv'); s=p.read_text(); s=s.replace('
# Use a version of OpenSSL that will be managed
[[ "$unamestr" == 'Darwin' ]] && export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)"
','
'); p.write_text(s)"

state.title: Attempts to remove deprecated OpenSSL line

parts.text:
I tried to apply it directly, but this session doesn’t have write permission to `/Users/mark/.zshenv` (permission denied), so I couldn’t save the file for you.

Run this locally to remove the bad line and reload zsh:

```zsh
cp ~/.zshenv ~/.zshenv.bak
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path.home() / ".zshenv"
lines = p.read_text().splitlines()
lines = [ln for ln in lines if "RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS" not in ln or "openssl@1.1" not in ln]
p.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n")
print("Updated", p)
PY
exec zsh
```

Quick verify:

```zsh
grep -n "openssl@1.1\|RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS" ~/.zshenv || echo "clean"
```

If you want, I can also give you a one-liner to set up `rbenv` with Ruby `3.3.6` next.

Nice — here’s a clean upgrade flow for `rbenv` + Ruby `3.3.6` on macOS.

```zsh
# 1) Install/update tooling
brew update
brew install rbenv ruby-build openssl@3 readline libyaml gmp

# 2) Ensure rbenv is initialized in zsh (if not already)
grep -q 'rbenv init -' ~/.zshrc || echo 'eval "$(rbenv init - zsh)"' >> ~/.zshrc
exec zsh

# 3) Build and install Ruby 3.3.6 with OpenSSL 3
RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@3) --with-readline-dir=$(brew --prefix readline) --with-libyaml-dir=$(brew --prefix libyaml) --with-gmp-dir=$(brew --prefix gmp)" \
rbenv install 3.3.6

# 4) Set as global default
rbenv global 3.3.6
rbenv rehash

# 5) Verify
ruby -v
which ruby
gem env home
```

Expected checks:
- `ruby -v` shows `ruby 3.3.6...`
- `which ruby` points to `~/.rbenv/shims/ruby`

Optional cleanup (after you confirm everything works):
```zsh
rbenv versions
# if 2.6.10 is no longer needed:
rbenv uninstall 2.6.10
```

If `rbenv install 3.3.6` fails, paste the last ~40 lines and I’ll pinpoint the fix quickly.
